agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering expedited courier service with a target 2-hour delivery window, premium pricing, and a conditional refund policy for missed windows. Stakeholder notes reveal significant contradictions between marketing intent, legal constraints, and operational realities that must be resolved before implementation.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery — a sub-domain of Order Fulfillment that governs eligibility, dispatch, SLA tracking, and refund processing for time-sensitive deliveries",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option with a target 2-hour delivery window from order placement to doorstep, available to eligible customers within a defined service radius" },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee when the customer selects Express Delivery" },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge, which is never refunded under the Express SLA refund policy" },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour target timeframe beginning at order placement within which the delivery should be completed" },
      { "term": "Service Radius", "definition": "The maximum distance (10km) between the customer's delivery address and the nearest warehouse for Express eligibility" },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute internal operational target for a driver to physically collect the package from the warehouse after order placement" },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above who is qualified to accept Express Delivery assignments" },
      { "term": "Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute target to assign a replacement driver when the original driver cancels mid-delivery" },
      { "term": "SLA Breach", "definition": "The event triggered when a delivery exceeds the 2-hour window, initiating the refund evaluation process" },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A discretionary (non-contractual) refund of the Express Fee issued when an SLA breach occurs and the customer is not at fault" },
      { "term": "Customer-Fault Exception", "definition": "A condition (e.g., wrong address provided by customer) that disqualifies a delivery from the goodwill refund, even if the SLA was breached" }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express Delivery is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of an active warehouse",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — if the customer is beyond 10km, the 2-hour window is operationally impossible. Allowing orders outside this radius corrupts the service promise.",
      "testable_assertion": "The system must reject Express Delivery selection when the geocoded delivery address is more than 10km from the nearest active warehouse"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The Express Fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can be adjusted by Finance or Product without corrupting system state",
      "testable_assertion": "When a customer selects Express Delivery, the order total includes the base delivery fee plus exactly $12.99 as the express surcharge"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "An Express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational target/threshold that could be tuned based on capacity — not a data integrity constraint",
      "testable_assertion": "If no driver has picked up the order within 15 minutes of placement, the system raises a Pickup SLA Breach alert to dispatch operations"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned Express deliveries",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Quality threshold that Operations could adjust (e.g., 3.5 stars during peak demand) without corrupting system integrity",
      "testable_assertion": "The dispatch system must never assign an Express order to a driver whose current rating is below 4.0 stars"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When an SLA breach occurs and the customer is not at fault, the Express Fee (not the base fee) is refunded as a goodwill gesture",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Refund policy is a business decision that can change. The invariant is that the refund never exceeds what was charged.",
      "testable_assertion": "Upon SLA breach with no customer-fault exception, a refund of exactly the Express Fee amount is initiated; the base delivery fee remains charged"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No refund is issued if the SLA breach was caused by the customer providing an incorrect delivery address",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Fault-attribution policy that could be expanded or relaxed by Customer Success",
      "testable_assertion": "When an SLA breach occurs and the delivery record is flagged with a customer-fault reason of 'wrong address', no automatic refund is initiated"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement Express-eligible driver must be assigned within 10 minutes or an automatic refund is triggered",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational recovery policy with a tunable time window",
      "testable_assertion": "When a driver cancels an in-progress Express delivery and no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes, the system automatically initiates an Express Fee refund"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "A goodwill refund must never exceed the Express Fee amount charged on that order",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Financial integrity constraint — refunding more than what was charged for the express service would be a data/accounting error",
      "testable_assertion": "The refund amount for an Express SLA breach must equal the Express Fee on the original order and must never include the base delivery fee"
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "Express delivery real-time tracking must be available to the customer from the moment a driver is assigned until delivery completion",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint",
      "testable_assertion": "Once a driver is assigned to an Express order, the customer-facing tracking endpoint returns live driver location updates until the delivery status is 'completed' or 'cancelled'"
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse (boundary condition)", "expected_behavior": "Must define whether 10km is inclusive or exclusive. Recommend inclusive (<=10km) to avoid customer confusion at the boundary." },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4.0 stars while en route on an Express delivery", "expected_behavior": "The delivery should be completed by the current driver — the rating threshold applies at assignment time, not during transit. Revoking mid-delivery would harm the customer." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at minute 115 of a 120-minute window (near SLA expiry)", "expected_behavior": "The 10-minute replacement window would extend past the 2-hour SLA. The system should still attempt replacement but auto-refund if the SLA is breached regardless of whether replacement succeeds." },
    { "scenario": "Customer provides a wrong address but corrects it within 5 minutes", "expected_behavior": "Unclear whether the 'wrong address' fault exception still applies after correction. Needs business decision on whether the correction resets the clock or if original fault stands." },
    { "scenario": "No Express-eligible drivers available at order placement", "expected_behavior": "The system should not allow Express selection if no eligible drivers are available in the service area, or clearly communicate expected delay." },
    { "scenario": "Warehouse is temporarily closed or out of stock for Express", "expected_behavior": "Express option should be suppressed. The 10km eligibility check alone is insufficient — warehouse operational status must also gate availability." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple SLA breach triggers on the same order (e.g., driver cancels AND 2-hour window exceeded)", "expected_behavior": "Only one refund of the Express Fee should be issued per order, regardless of how many breach conditions are triggered. Idempotent refund processing." },
    { "scenario": "Customer disputes 'wrong address' determination", "expected_behavior": "Need a dispute/appeals process. Who adjudicates? Customer Success manually, or is there an automated check (e.g., geocoding confidence score)?" },
    { "scenario": "Refund processing falls on a weekend or holiday (3 business days rule)", "expected_behavior": "The 3-business-day clock excludes weekends and holidays. Must define which holiday calendar applies." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express Fee at $12.99 creates a new revenue stream but carries refund liability. Unclear refund rate expectations — if SLA breach rate exceeds ~15-20%, the operational cost of Express (priority dispatch, driver premiums) may erode margins. The 'goodwill vs guarantee' distinction from Legal may reduce refund volume if enforced, but damages customer trust if perceived as bait-and-switch.",
    "operational": "15-minute pickup SLA and 4-star driver requirement significantly constrain the driver pool. During peak hours or low-supply periods, Express availability may be severely limited. The 10-minute replacement window requires a sophisticated real-time dispatch system with driver pool monitoring. Operations needs tooling for SLA breach monitoring, fault attribution, and refund initiation.",
    "user": "Real-time tracking and a 2-hour window are strong value propositions. However, the Legal constraint against calling it a 'guarantee' conflicts with Customer Success wanting to show it 'prominently.' Customers who pay $12.99 expecting a guarantee but receive 'best effort' will generate complaints and negative reviews. The wrong-address exception could feel punitive if not handled with empathy.",
    "risk": "HIGH — The fundamental contradiction between marketing the service as a 'guarantee' and Legal's insistence on 'best effort' creates regulatory, reputational, and litigation risk. If the product launches with 'guaranteed' language and refunds are then denied as 'goodwill,' this could trigger consumer protection scrutiny. Additionally, under-staffing Express-eligible drivers risks chronic SLA breaches that undermine the product before it gains traction.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Pay a premium for speed; experience varies based on SLA language clarity, driver availability, and refund process transparency" },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "Only 4-star+ drivers qualify, creating a two-tier system. Express drivers may expect premium pay. Rating pressure intensifies." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "15-minute pickup SLA requires Express orders to be prioritized in picking/packing queues, potentially delaying standard orders" },
      { "group": "Customer Success", "impact": "Will handle disputes around wrong-address exceptions, refund timing, and the guarantee-vs-best-effort gap. Need clear playbooks." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "Must build refund processing within 3-business-day SLA, track Express Fee revenue vs refund liability, and report on Express margin" },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must review all customer-facing language to ensure no binding guarantee is implied. Refund terms must be in T&Cs." },
      { "group": "Product/Engineering", "impact": "Must build eligibility checks, real-time tracking, SLA monitoring, automated refund triggers, and driver replacement dispatch — significant new system complexity" }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 35,
    "reasoning": "The spec contains a fundamental unresolved contradiction between Product/Customer Success (who call this a 'guarantee') and Legal (who insist it is 'best effort'). This is not a terminology quibble — it changes the entire product promise, refund policy enforcement, UI language, and customer expectations. Until this is resolved, acceptance criteria cannot be reliably defined. Additionally, the refund trigger logic has conflicting descriptions (automatic vs 3-business-day processing vs goodwill discretion).",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "10km service radius as an eligibility gate",
      "Express Fee amount of $12.99",
      "Base delivery fee is never refunded under Express SLA policy",
      "4-star minimum driver rating for Express eligibility",
      "Real-time tracking requirement"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort — this changes everything downstream",
      "Whether refunds are automatic or discretionary (goodwill)",
      "Whether the 3-business-day processing window applies to all refunds or just manual ones",
      "What constitutes 'wrong address' and who adjudicates",
      "Whether the 10-minute replacement window refund is truly automatic or also subject to goodwill discretion",
      "How Express interacts with standard order fulfillment priority"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement, not at driver pickup",
      "The 10km distance is calculated as straight-line (geodesic), not driving distance",
      "Driver rating is their current cumulative rating, not a rolling window",
      "Only one warehouse needs to be within 10km, not all warehouses",
      "Express Delivery applies to the same item catalog as standard delivery"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Product Manager and Customer Success describe the 2-hour window as a 'guarantee' that should be shown 'prominently,' while Legal explicitly states it cannot be a guarantee in writing and is 'best effort' only. This contradiction is foundational — it affects UI copy, refund policy enforcement, customer expectations, and potential regulatory exposure. These cannot coexist.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Customer Success", "Product"],
      "options": [
        "A: Position as 'guarantee' with automatic refunds — accept legal risk, align with PM/CS vision",
        "B: Position as 'best effort target' with discretionary goodwill refunds — align with Legal, risk customer disappointment",
        "C: Position as 'Express Promise' with automatic refunds but T&C language that avoids contractual obligation — hybrid approach requiring Legal sign-off on specific wording"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — a middle path where the product experience feels like a guarantee (automatic refunds, prominent display) but the legal language in T&Cs frames it as a voluntary commitment. However, this MUST be validated by Legal before any implementation begins."
    },
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Refund trigger mechanism is contradictory: Customer Success says refunds should be 'automatic' when a replacement driver isn't found within 10 minutes. Finance says refunds are 'processed within 3 business days.' Legal says refunds are 'goodwill gestures' (implying discretion). These three models (automatic/delayed-processing/discretionary) are mutually incompatible and must be reconciled into a single refund workflow.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Finance", "Customer Success", "Engineering"],
      "options": [
        "A: Fully automatic — system triggers refund instantly upon SLA breach (Finance's 3-day window becomes processing time, not decision time)",
        "B: Auto-initiated, manually approved — system flags breach, CS reviews within 3 business days, approves/denies",
        "C: Hybrid — auto-refund for clear-cut cases (driver cancellation, no replacement), manual review for ambiguous cases (wrong address disputes)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — automatic refunds for unambiguous breaches (driver cancellation without replacement) with manual review for fault-disputed cases. The 3-business-day window applies to manual review cases only. This satisfies Customer Success's speed expectation while preserving Finance's processing timeline for edge cases."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The 'wrong address' customer-fault exception lacks definition. What constitutes a 'wrong' address? A typo in the apartment number? A completely different city? What if the address is technically correct but the customer isn't there? Who determines fault — the driver, an algorithm, Customer Success? Without clear fault-attribution rules, this exception will be inconsistently applied and generate disputes.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Customer Success", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Narrow definition — only applies when geocoding fails entirely or address doesn't exist",
        "B: Broad definition — any address issue including inaccessible locations, wrong unit numbers, customer not present",
        "C: Tiered definition — auto-deny for non-existent addresses, manual review for ambiguous cases"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — but this needs explicit documentation and a dispute resolution process before launch"
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No information provided about: (1) What happens to an Express order if the pickup SLA (15 min) is breached — is it downgraded to standard? Auto-refunded? (2) Whether Express-eligible drivers receive premium compensation. (3) How Express orders are prioritized vs standard orders in warehouse picking queues. (4) Maximum number of concurrent Express orders per warehouse. (5) Operating hours for Express service. These gaps affect system design and business viability.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Warehouse Operations", "Drivers", "Product/Engineering", "Finance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Proceed with assumptions and refine post-launch",
        "B: Conduct a follow-up discovery session focused on operational workflow before implementation"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B — the operational workflow gaps (especially pickup SLA breach handling and warehouse prioritization) are critical path items that will block implementation regardless. Better to resolve now than discover mid-sprint."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "The product cannot launch with 'guaranteed 2-hour delivery' language until Legal signs off on the exact wording. Implementing a 'guarantee' UI without legal approval creates regulatory and litigation risk.", "business_rule_broken": "Legal constraint on SLA language" },
    { "violation": "The refund workflow cannot be implemented until the automatic vs discretionary contradiction is resolved. Building the wrong model would require re-architecture.", "business_rule_broken": "Conflicting refund trigger mechanisms across BR-5, BR-7, and Legal's goodwill framing" }
  ]
}
